reasoning: The question asks whether seasonally adjusted overall commercial bank deposits will have increased over a roughly 3-month horizon (from late October to late January). Historically, nominal commercial bank deposits feature a strong upward trend dictated by M2 expansion and nominal GDP growth, with a standard quarterly increase of ~1-2%. A positive change over a 12-week or 13-week period happens in approximately 80-85% of non-QT periods. While the 2022-2023 tightening cycle constrained deposit growth, by late 2025 it is highly probable the Federal Reserve will have ceased Quantitative Tightening, allowing deposits to resume their normal expansion. Hence, I assign a 75% probability to an increase over the 3-month period.
